commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: If, then, this God, to whom we bear witness, be as He is, the God of hope, we acknowledge our hope, speeding on to hope, "saturated with goodness, filled with all knowledge."

commentaries.author: Gennadius of Constantinople
commentaries.content: By “the God of all hope” Paul means that God has blessed us with the hope of things to come. .

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: And again: "Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, by the power of the Holy Spirit."

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: In other words, may you get rid of your heartlessness toward one another and not be cast down by temptations. You will achieve this by abounding in hope, which is the cause of all good things and comes from the Holy Spirit. It is not just from the Spirit, though, because you must do your part also. That is why Paul adds the words “in believing.”

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
